methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $39M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Liminal Insights is a Series A climate and energy technology company founded by Barry Van Tassell and Shaurjo Biswas. The target has raised $38.9M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-stage, same-sector private companies (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M) alongside one public comparable, with additional reference to later-stage private peers including TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials.

Two triangulation methods apply here. The stage-progression method, using the $39M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yields an implied valuation of $78M. The peer-median method anchors to the three direct comparables, which trade at a median valuation of $150M with a range spanning $110M to $200M, directly implying $150M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $66M to $172M, with a median of $114M. The low end reflects stage-based progression from capital deployed; the high end tracks the upper quartile of peer multiples. Key uncertainties include differentiation within the peer set—the $90M spread between Steady Energy and Graphyte reflects substantial dispersion in early-stage climate tech valuations—and the limited sample size of three direct comparables. Revenue traction, capital efficiency, and technology defensibility will materially affect positioning within this range.
